_README: Make Scenario Blueprint — Phase 2: Draft-Reply Engine. Import AFTER Phase 1 is stable and the SOP sheet audit with Britney is complete. This scenario is triggered by a webhook from Phase 1 (or can run on a schedule polling Score 3 rows in the master log). One scenario handles all talents — it dynamically looks up the correct SOP tab. DRAFT MODE ACTIVE: replies are saved as Gmail Drafts for supervisor review — nothing is sent automatically until Marco approves full autonomous operation (~1 month trial).
_import_instructions: 1. Complete the SOP sheet audit with Britney FIRST. All talent tabs must match sheets/sop_matrix_template.csv.
3. Fill in all TODO values below.
4. DRAFT MODE: replies are saved as Gmail Drafts, not sent. Marco reviews drafts in Gmail before approving autonomous send.
5. To enable auto-send after trial: replace gmail:CreateDraftReply (step 10) with gmail:ReplyToThread and restore the sleep delay (step 9). Update config/settings.json → reply.draft_mode to false.
_architecture_note: This scenario reads 'queued for reply' rows from the master log (Score 3, Reply Sent = FALSE), processes them, saves a Gmail Draft reply, then updates the log row. Running on a schedule (every 5 minutes) is the recommended approach during Phase 2 to keep it simple. DRAFT MODE: no emails are sent automatically — all replies land in the talent's Gmail Drafts folder for Marco to review.
name: Phase 2 — Draft-Reply Engine

parameters._spreadsheetId_comment: TODO: Fill in the master log Google Sheet ID.
parameters._limit_comment: Process up to 10 queued emails per run. Adjust as needed.

parameters._spreadsheetId_comment: TODO: Fill in Britney's SOP matrix Google Sheet ID.
parameters._sheetName_comment: Dynamically reads the tab named after the talent. Tab names must exactly match the Talent Name column in the master log.
parameters._range_comment: Reads columns A through F: Offer Type, Minimum Rate, Response Template, Auto-Respond Flag, Brand Blacklist, Special Rules.

parameters.name: sop_row
parameters._comment: Finds the row in the SOP sheet where column A (Offer Type) matches the email's offer type detected during triage. If no match, falls back to 'Other' row.
parameters._fallback_comment: TODO: Add a fallback filter for 'Other' offer type if the primary match returns empty.

routes._label: Auto-Respond Flag = ESCALATE or NO — route to Score 2 behavior

values.Notes: Auto-Respond Flag = {{3.sop_row[3]}} — routed to human review

routes._label: Auto-Respond Flag = YES — proceed to draft and send reply

parameters._model_comment: Always use gpt-4o for reply drafting. Do NOT substitute gpt-4o-mini here.

messages.content:
You are a professional talent management assistant drafting email replies on behalf of talent.

Your job is to fill in the provided SOP response template using details from the original email.
Write as if you are the talent's team — professional, warm, and concise.

Rules:
- Replace ALL placeholders: [BRAND_NAME], [TALENT_NAME], [OFFER_TYPE], [PROPOSED_RATE], [MINIMUM_RATE]
- If a placeholder value is unknown, replace it with a natural phrase
- Do NOT add commentary, disclaimers, or AI-related text outside the email
- Do NOT invent specifics not present in the original email
- Output ONLY the finished email reply text, ready to send. No subject line. No preamble.

If the SOP template or talent rules indicate ESCALATE for this offer type, output exactly:
ESCALATE: <one-sentence reason>

parameters._note: TODO: In production, fetch the original email body from Gmail using the thread ID and inject it into the user message above. The log stores the thread link but not the full body. Add a gmail:GetEmail module before this step and pass the body text into this prompt.

routes._label: GPT returned ESCALATE — route to human
Reply drafted — save as Gmail Draft for supervisor review

parameters._connection_comment: TODO: Make connection names for each talent Gmail must be in format 'Gmail - [TalentFirstName]' and match exactly the Talent Name in the log. This dynamic lookup requires all connections to be consistently named.
parameters._threadId_comment: Extracts the thread ID from the Gmail link stored in the log.
parameters._bodyType_comment: Send as plain text. Change to 'html' only if SOP templates contain HTML formatting.
parameters._draft_mode_note: DRAFT MODE: saves as a Gmail Draft reply — nothing is sent. Marco reviews drafts in Gmail. To switch to live send: replace this module with gmail:ReplyToThread (same parameters) and update config/settings.json → reply.draft_mode to false.

values.Notes: Draft saved at {{now}} — awaiting Marco review before send

metadata._instant_comment: false = runs on a schedule (every 5 minutes recommended during Phase 2). Not webhook-based.

scenario._sequential_comment: true = emails are processed one at a time to avoid race conditions when updating log rows.

metadata._zone_comment: TODO: Update to your Make workspace zone.

_TODO_checklist: Complete SOP sheet audit with Britney BEFORE activating this scenario
All SOP tab names must exactly match talent names in the master log (case-sensitive)
Replace [MASTER_LOG_SHEET_ID] with the master log Sheet ID
Replace [SOP_MATRIX_SHEET_ID] with Britney's SOP matrix Sheet ID
Set Gmail connection names in Make to exactly 'Gmail - [TalentFirstName]' format
Add a gmail:GetEmail module before step 6 to inject the original email body into the GPT reply prompt (see TODO note in step 6)
DRAFT MODE is active — replies are saved as Gmail Drafts, NOT sent. Marco reviews drafts in the talent Gmail inbox.

After ~1 month trial and Marco's approval: replace gmail:CreateDraftReply (step 10) with gmail:ReplyToThread, restore the sleep delay (step 9, 900 seconds), and update config/settings.json → reply.draft_mode to false.
Test on Katrina's inbox (katrina@taboost.me) first — manually review every saved draft before approving broader rollout
